trivia_bullets.text: Kor Castigator is a common Creature — Kor Wizard Ally for {1}{W} from Battle for Zendikar (BFZ, October 2015). The Ally subtype is the BFZ-block's mechanical encoding of the Zendikari multi-race resistance coalition: Allies trigger effects off each other entering the battlefield, representing coordinated military action among kor, humans, merfolk, elves, and goblins organized against the Eldrazi brood.
sources: MTG Wiki: Allies mechanic, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: A 3/1 that cannot be blocked by Eldrazi Scions; pauper- and commander-legal. The Scion-evasion is bespoke anti-Eldrazi design: Eldrazi Scions are the 1/1 token sacrifice-for-mana fodder the BFZ Eldrazi decks generate as ramp. A 3/1 that slides past the Scion defensive wall mechanically represents what the flavor text describes — a kor warrior who walks into a swarm of Eldrazi and passes through unimpeded.

"He told us not to worry, and he walked into the cavern. The swirling mass of Eldrazi parted to admit him, then closed in his wake." — Akiri, kor line-slinger. The speaker is Akiri, canonically the greatest line-slinger on Zendikar and a survivor of Sea Gate's fall. Akiri later became a Commander-legal legendary card (Akiri, Line-Slinger C16; Akiri, Fearless Voyager ZNR). The flavor text positions Akiri as witness, not the subject — the unnamed kor Castigator is the actor.

Community sentiment: Kor Castigator's Eldrazi-Scion-evasion is considered a very narrow BFZ-draft-specific ability that aged poorly outside that format's Eldrazi-heavy context. The 3/1 body for {1}{W} is aggressive but fragile. No broader constructed impact noted across pauper or commander community coverage.
sources: general community consensus, no specific thread cited
